content: Always separate cross-module consumer logic into a testable process_* function (pool, event_id, payload) and a thin NATS loop wrapper — test the process function directly against real Postgres
Consumer crates must define their own local payload structs with Deserialize rather than importing from the producer crate — the contract is the JSON envelope, not a Rust type
When processing multi-item events, build deterministic idempotency keys as format!("{prefix}-{event_id}-{item_index}") so each item gets its own replay-safe key

When a receipt cost is derived from prior transactions (e.g., component issue costs), guard against zero or empty cost data before creating the receipt — never allow phantom entries with no cost basis
In Docker Compose health checks, always use 127.0.0.1 instead of localhost — Alpine wget resolves localhost to IPv6 first, which fails if the service only binds IPv4
Cross-module workflows use the command-request pattern: producer enqueues a .requested event via outbox, consumer processes it independently — never call the consuming module API directly
When adding subtypes to an existing entity (e.g., in-process vs final inspection), extend the table with nullable columns and a type discriminator rather than creating separate tables
